items.title: Blood test flags lung-cancer risk up to 5 years early via 14-protein signature
items.summary: Researchers reported a 14-protein blood signature, identified with machine learning on plasma from 48,000+ people and validated across eight datasets, that predicts lung-cancer risk years before diagnosis by detecting an altered inflammatory lung environment.
items.why: Another proof point that predictive multi-biomarker blood panels — the heart of Protocol's screening proposition — keep advancing toward earlier risk detection. A future SKU candidate and a tailwind for the ‘screen earlier’ narrative.

items.title: GRAIL's Galleri multi-cancer test: NHS trial reappraised after ‘negative’ headline
items.summary: An oncologist op-ed in STAT unpacked the first RCT of an MCED test (NHS-Galleri): ~99.55% specificity, 52% PPV, 54.7% sensitivity for 12 prespecified cancers (30.7% across all), arguing the ‘negative’ framing misses real signal on stage-shift and feasibility at national scale.
items.why: MCED is the exact category Protocol's SPOT-MAS line sits in. How the evidence debate resolves directly shapes how aggressively Protocol can position multi-cancer screening claims to patients.

items.title: US Medicare GLP-1 ‘Bridge’ demonstration to begin July 2026
items.summary: Medicare confirmed a weight-loss-drug demonstration starting July 2026: Ozempic, Wegovy, Mounjaro and Zepbound priced at ~US$245/month with beneficiary copays of ~US$50/month.
items.why: Reinforces the GLP-1 price-compression risk already flagged in Protocol's competitor brief. Government-anchored pricing pulls the global reference point down further — pressure on the weight-management AOV assumption.

items.title: Wellness Index Report: demand shifting from ‘longevity’ to root-cause health
items.summary: A new Wellness Index Report flags an evolution in consumer priorities — growing use of personalized diagnostics to uncover root causes, rising demand for non-pharma pain management, and more emphasis on human connection and mental wellness.
items.why: Validates demand for diagnostics-led, root-cause care (Protocol's model) but signals the messaging frame is moving beyond the ‘longevity’ label — worth testing in CHT positioning.
